title: French Beef Empanadas
description: A French twist on the classic Argentine empanada, filled with tender beef and seasoned with herbs.
instructions: In a large skillet, cook the ground beef over medium-high heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the onion and garlic to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked beef mixture, flour, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Use a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out circles of dough.
Place a tablespoon of the beef mixture in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half over the filling and press the edges together with a fork to seal.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the beaten egg and cut a few slits in the top of each to allow steam to escape.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
